mechanism_narrative: After a major recession, the first month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a durable economic recovery has begun. This positive confirmation of a labor market bottom boosts investor confidence and drives equity returns higher over the medium term as future consumption and earnings expectations are revised up.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC labor market collapse with UNRATE at 9.4%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS monthly change turns positive for the first time after a recessionary period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained momentum in industrial production, measured by its 3-month rate of change, reflects improving corporate fundamentals and aggregate supply. This is a leading indicator of earnings growth, positively impacting equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO shows a rank information coefficient below 0.05 with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate policy (ZIRP) environment, rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) is not met with monetary tightening. This combination signals deeply negative real rates, forcing a portfolio shift from cash and bonds into equities and driving short-term positive returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has communicated ZIRP for an extended period
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the correlation between monthly CPIAUCSL changes and 20-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer demand, which is a primary driver of corporate revenues. This negative signal leads to downward revisions of earnings expectations and negative equity returns over the very short term.
failure_condition: In triggered instances (RSAFS 1-month change < -0.5%), S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) peaks and begins to decline, it confirms that the worst of the recession is over and a durable recovery has begun. This is a powerful long-term positive signal for equities as it implies future growth in consumption and corporate profits.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change < 0 after a prior 12-month rise of > 2pp
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is at multi-decade highs of 9.4%
failure_condition: Following a confirmed peak in UNRATE, the S&P 500 fails to produce positive returns over the subsequent 60-day period in more than 40% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts (HOUST) from depressed levels signals a bottoming of the housing market, which was the epicenter of the crisis. This stabilization reduces systemic risk and improves bank balance sheets and consumer wealth, providing a positive impulse for the broader equity market.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market collapse was central to the 2008 crisis
failure_condition: When the HOUST 3-month moving average crosses above the 6-month moving average from below, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the quarter-over-quarter growth rate of real GDP (GDPC1) provides the most comprehensive signal of improving economic activity. This directly translates into higher expected corporate earnings and justifies higher equity valuations over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Economy is emerging from a severe recession with negative GDP growth
failure_condition: The rank correlation between quarterly GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive (p > 0.10) over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) and decelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals stagflationary pressure. This environment erodes corporate profit margins via rising input costs and falling output, leading to negative equity performance.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.
